tool.name: fetch_js_files
tool.description:
Fetch all JavaScript files from a webpage.
Extracts both external JS file references and inline script content. This is the first step in JavaScript reconnaissance - gathering all JS assets for analysis.
Use Cases: - Enumerate all JavaScript loaded by a web application - Prepare for endpoint extraction and secret scanning - Understand the client-side attack surface
Next Steps After This Tool: - Use extract_endpoints to find API calls - Use extract_secrets to find credentials - Use analyze_js_frameworks to understand the tech stack

parameters.parameter: Use Playwright for dynamic JS extraction (slower but captures dynamically loaded scripts)
Maximum number of external JS files to fetch
Timeout per HTTP request in seconds

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - success: Whether fetch succeeded - url: The target URL - js_files: List of JS file objects (url, content, size) - js_file_count: Number of external JS files - inline_scripts: List of inline script contents - inline_count: Number of inline scripts - total_size_kb: Total JS content size in KB - errors: Any fetch errors encountered
tool.example: fetch_js_files(url="https://spa.example.com") → Returns all JS files and inline scripts from the page

tool.name: extract_endpoints
tool.description:
Extract API endpoints from JavaScript content.
Parses JavaScript for REST API calls (fetch, axios, XMLHttpRequest), GraphQL endpoints, WebSocket URLs, and other backend communication patterns.
This is a CRITICAL reconnaissance step - discovered endpoints become attack surfaces for fuzzing, injection testing, and IDOR discovery.
Patterns Detected: - fetch() and axios calls - XMLHttpRequest.open() calls - jQuery $.ajax(), $.get(), $.post() - Express/Koa route definitions - URL constants and path patterns - WebSocket connections - GraphQL endpoints
Use Cases: - Discover hidden API endpoints not in documentation - Find authenticated endpoints for access control testing - Enumerate parameters for fuzzing - Identify GraphQL schemas for introspection attacks

parameters.parameter: JavaScript content to analyze (provide this OR url)
URL to fetch and analyze (if content not provided)
Also extract parameter names from requests

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - success: Whether extraction succeeded - endpoints: List of endpoints (url, method, source_pattern, parameters, is_authenticated) - endpoint_count: Number of endpoints found - parameters: List of discovered parameter names - auth_patterns: Detected authentication patterns

tool.name: analyze_secrets
tool.description:
Detect potential secrets, API keys, and credentials in JavaScript.
Scans for known secret patterns including: - AWS Access Keys and Secret Keys - Google API Keys and OAuth tokens - GitHub tokens - Stripe API keys - Slack tokens and webhooks - JWT tokens - Generic API keys and passwords - Base64 encoded secrets - Private key material
SECURITY WARNING: This tool may expose sensitive information. Handle results carefully and report responsibly.
Use Cases: - Find hardcoded credentials for privilege escalation - Discover API keys for third-party service abuse - Identify JWT tokens for signature bypass attacks - Find private keys for authentication bypass
Severity Levels: - high: Known credential formats (AWS, Stripe, etc.) - medium: Generic secrets and API keys - low: Potential secrets in test/example contexts

parameters.parameter: JavaScript content to analyze (provide this OR url)

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - success: Whether scan completed - secrets: List of secrets (type, description, value_preview, severity) - secret_count: Total number of secrets found - high_count: Number of high-severity findings - severity_breakdown: Count by severity level
tool.example: analyze_secrets(url="https://example.com/config.js", severity_filter="high") → Returns high-severity secrets like AWS keys, Stripe keys

tool.name: analyze_js_frameworks
tool.description:
Detect frontend frameworks and extract routing patterns.
Identifies JavaScript frameworks and libraries: - React (including React Router, hooks) - Angular (routing, HttpClient) - Vue.js (Vue Router) - Next.js (pages, API routes) - Nuxt.js - jQuery - Axios - GraphQL (Apollo, etc.)
Also extracts: - Frontend routes (for testing access control) - API patterns specific to framework conventions
Use Cases: - Understand the application architecture - Find framework-specific vulnerabilities - Discover hidden frontend routes - Identify API conventions to predict endpoints

parameters.parameter: JavaScript content to analyze (provide this OR url)

tool.returns: Dictionary with: - success: Whether analysis completed - frameworks: List of detected frameworks (name, confidence, routes) - primary_framework: Most likely primary framework - routes: All extracted frontend routes - api_patterns: Framework-specific API patterns

tool.name: comprehensive_js_analysis
tool.description:
Perform comprehensive JavaScript analysis on a target URL.
This is the RECOMMENDED entry point for JS reconnaissance. It orchestrates the full pipeline:
1. Fetch all JS files (external and inline) 2. Extract API endpoints for fuzzing 3. Detect secrets and credentials 4. Identify frameworks and routing 5. Extract parameters for injection testing
Use This When: - Starting reconnaissance on a web application - You need a complete picture of the JS attack surface - You want to feed endpoints into the hypothesis ledger - You're looking for quick wins (secrets, hidden endpoints)
After This Tool: - Add discovered endpoints to coverage tracker - Create hypotheses for interesting endpoints - Fuzz parameters with injection payloads - Test authentication on protected endpoints

parameters.parameter: Maximum external JS files to fetch
Scan for secrets (may expose sensitive data)

tool.returns: Comprehensive results including: - js_files_found: Number of JS files analyzed - endpoints: All extracted API endpoints - parameters: All discovered parameters - auth_patterns: Authentication patterns found - frameworks: Detected frameworks - routes: Frontend routes - secrets: Potential secrets (if include_secrets=true) - high_severity_secrets: Count of critical findings
tool.example: comprehensive_js_analysis(url="https://banking-app.example.com") → Complete JS reconnaissance including endpoints, secrets, and framework info
